service: Dog and Cat Registration and Anti-Rabies Vaccination (ARV)

detailedRequirements.requirement: Dog or cat must be 3 months old and above, and healthy
No history of medication or animal bite/scratch incident for the past 2 weeks

clientSteps.action: Visit the Office and request for ARV and pet registration.
Present and handle the cat/dog to be vaccinated.
Claim the Certificate of Vaccination.

plainLanguageName: Register your pet and get anti-rabies vaccination

service: Animal Control (Stray Cats and Stray Dogs)

detailedRequirements.requirement: For private subdivision or within a private property: Request letter addressed to the Office of the Municipal Mayor

clientSteps.action: Submit a request letter to the Office of the Municipal Mayor.
Present the request letter received by the Office of the Municipal Mayor.
Assist the animal control team during the operation.

plainLanguageName: Report and have stray animals controlled

service: Mass Anti-Rabies Vaccination for Cats and Dogs

detailedRequirements.requirement: Request letter addressed to the Municipal Mayor

clientSteps.action: Submit the request letter to the Office of the Municipal Mayor.
Provide a covered venue and assist the Vaccination team during the activity.

plainLanguageName: Request a mass anti-rabies vaccination drive

service: Issuance of Veterinary Health Certificate for Travel Purpose of Cats and Dogs

detailedRequirements.requirement: Cat or dog must be vaccinated 14 days prior to departure (anti-rabies, not expired)
Cat or dog must be healthy and at least 18 months old

clientSteps.action: Bring your pet and vaccination record.
Request for Veterinary Health Certificate for Travel Purpose.
Present the pet for physical assessment.
Proceed to the Treasury Office to pay the certification fee (Php 130.00).
Present the Official Receipt to the Agriculture Office and claim the Certificate.

plainLanguageName: Get a health certificate for your pet before travel

service: Gulayan sa Paaralan or Community Garden

detailedRequirements.requirement: Request letter addressed to the Municipal Mayor

clientSteps.action: Submit a request letter to the Office of the Municipal Mayor.
Assist the technician during the field visitation and ocular inspection.
Participate actively in the scheduled seminar/training.

plainLanguageName: Get support for a school or community vegetable garden

service: Redemption of Impounded Animals

clientSteps.action: Request to redeem the impounded animal; provide proof of ownership and government-issued ID.
Fill up the Release, Waiver and Quitclaim Form.
Pay the penalty fee at the Treasury Office.
Present the Official Receipt to the Agriculture Office to claim the animal.

plainLanguageName: Reclaim your impounded animal

service: Adoption of Impounded Animal

detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: Department of Agriculture – Bureau of Animal Industry

clientSteps.action: Request for adoption of an impounded animal.
Present government-issued identification.
For shelter/groups: provide shelter permit.
Fill up the Release, Waiver and Quitclaim Form.
Bring the animal cage to the impounding facility.

plainLanguageName: Adopt an impounded animal

service: Surrendering of Cats and Dogs with Owner

detailedRequirements.requirement: Declaration of Waiver and Liability Form

clientSteps.action: Request for Animal Surrender.
Present Government-Issued Identification.
Fill up the Animal Surrender Form, Euthanasia Consent Form, Declaration of Waiver and Liability Form, and Certificate of Abandonment.
Pay the Surrender fee (Php 500.00 per head) at the Treasury Office.
Present the Official Receipt.
Bring the animal to the impounding facility.

plainLanguageName: Formally surrender your cat or dog

service: Animal Health Services for Small Animals (Cats and Dogs)

clientSteps.action: Make a same-day appointment via call, social media, or personal visit.
Bring your pet on the given schedule for veterinary check-up.

plainLanguageName: Get a veterinary check-up for your cat or dog

service: Issuance of Veterinary Health Certificate for Travel Purpose of Poultry

detailedRequirements.requirement: Certificate of Free Status Avian Influenza Type A Subtypes H5 & H7
At least 2 weeks prior to travel date

clientSteps.action: Request for a Veterinary Health Certificate for Poultry.
Bring the birds with leg/wing bands, Newcastle Disease Vaccine, and Styrofoam box with ice on the scheduled date.
Submit collected blood and swab samples to the Bureau of Animal Industry in Quezon City on the same day of collection.
Once results are released, submit the Certificate of Free Status Avian Influenza Type A Subtypes H5 & H7 to the Agriculture Office.
Pay the certification fee (Php 130.00) at the Treasury Office.
Present the Official Receipt to the Agriculture Office.

plainLanguageName: Get a health certificate for poultry before travel

service: Animal Health Services for Livestock and Poultry (Checkup, Medication, Vaccination, Deworming, Sample Collection)

detailedRequirements.requirement: Certificate of Free Status Avian Influenza Type A Subtypes H5 & H7 (if applicable)

clientSteps.action: Request for Animal Health Service.
Report initial signs and observations.
Assist the veterinarian or technician during the farm visitation.

plainLanguageName: Get veterinary services for your livestock or poultry

service: Distribution of Assorted Vegetable Seeds and Seedlings

clientSteps.action: Request for assorted vegetable seeds.
Provide basic information: Name, Address, and size of planting area.

plainLanguageName: Get free vegetable seeds and seedlings

service: Annual Kangkong Registration

clientSteps.action: Request for kangkong registration.
Present government-issued identification.
Pay the kangkong registration permit fee (Php 500.00 per hectare).
Present the official receipt.

plainLanguageName: Annually register your kangkong farm

service: Registry System for Basic Sector in Agriculture (RSBSA) for Livestock, Poultry, High Value Crops and Rice

service: Damage Report for High Value Crops, Rice, Livestock and Poultry during Natural Calamity
whoMayAvail: Registered farmers affected by natural calamities

clientSteps.action: Immediately report the damage to the coordinator within 24 hours after the calamity.
Provide supporting documents of the damages.

processingTime: Initial report: 24 hours; Final report: 3 days after calamity
plainLanguageName: File a damage report after a natural calamity

service: Agricultural Trainings and Seminars

detailedRequirements.requirement: Request letter addressed to the Municipal Mayor

clientSteps.action: Submit the request letter to the Office of the Municipal Mayor.
Provide the necessary logistics if applicable.
Assist the coordinator and speakers during the event.

Assist the coordinator during the visitation if applicable.

service: Application for Transfer of Ownership

detailedRequirements.requirement: Deed of Absolute Sale / Deed of Donation / Extrajudicial Settlement, BIR CAR, Transfer Fee, Tax Clearance, Affidavit of Publication (for Extrajudicial Settlement), Special Power of Attorney, Secretary Certificate (if any)
Affidavit of Consolidation and Certificate of Sale (for Foreclosed Property)

supportingDocumentsDetail.note: Lack of any requirement will NOT be considered ACCEPTED.

clientSteps.action: Present and submit all required documents to the Assessor's Office.
Fill up Sworn Statement.
Pay corresponding fee (Php 200.00) at the Treasurer's Office.
Wait for processing and release of owner's copy of Tax Declaration (approximately 3 days).

plainLanguageName: Transfer a property's tax declaration to a new owner

service: Application for Appraisal and Assessment of New Buildings and Improvements

supportingDocumentsDetail.note: If not the land owner, support any proof (Deed of Sale, Deed of Usufruct, Letter of Consent, etc.).

clientSteps.action: Present and submit requirements for appraisal and assessment.
Wait for ocular inspection and FAAS preparation.
Wait for Provincial Assessor approval (within 2 weeks).
Receive owner's copy of Tax Declaration and FAAS.

fees.description: Php 250.00 (Sworn Statement: Php 100.00 + Notary: Php 100.00 + Documentary Stamps: Php 50.00)

plainLanguageName: Get your new building assessed for taxes

service: Issuance of Certified True Copy of Tax Declaration and Certified Copy of Tax Map

detailedRequirements.requirement: Authority from the owner if the requesting party is not the owner

clientSteps.action: Present updated Realty Tax Payment and fill up request slip / request letter.
Secure Order of Payment and pay at Treasurer's Office.
Receive Certified True Copy of Tax Declaration and/or Certified Tax Map.

fees.description: Php 100.00 per Tax Declaration + Php 30.00 Documentary Stamps; Php 100.00 per Tax Map

plainLanguageName: Get a certified copy of your tax declaration or tax map

service: Issuance of Certificate of No Property or Landholdings

detailedRequirements.requirement: Authority from the owner if the requesting party is not the owner

clientSteps.action: Present latest Realty Tax Payment and fill up request slip.
Secure Order of Payment and pay at Treasury Office.
Receive the approved certification.

plainLanguageName: Get a certificate proving you have no property

service: Application for Reclassification of Real Property

supportingDocumentsDetail.note: A duly approved zoning ordinance also serves as basis in the reclassification of lands.

clientSteps.action: Present all requirements and fill up Sworn Statement.
Pay corresponding fee at the Treasury Office.
Wait for ocular inspection and FAAS preparation.
Receive owner's copy of Tax Declaration after Provincial Assessor approval (~2 weeks).

fees.description: Php 200.00 (Sworn Statement: Php 100.00 + Notary: Php 50.00 + Documentary Stamps: Php 50.00)

plainLanguageName: Reclassify your property's land use

service: Application for Lot Segregation or Consolidation

clientSteps.action: Present all requirements and fill up Sworn Statement.
Pay corresponding fee at the Treasury Office.
Wait for tax mapping and encoding of subdivided/consolidated parcels.
Receive new Tax Declarations and Notice of Assessment.

plainLanguageName: Subdivide or consolidate your land's tax declaration

service: Application for Newly Acquired Machineries

detailedRequirements.requirement: Duly certified copy of itemized list of machineries
Original cost and date of operation/acquisition of machineries

clientSteps.action: Present all requirements and fill up Sworn Statement.
Pay corresponding fee at the Treasury Office.
Wait for ocular inspection and FAAS preparation.
Receive Tax Declaration and Notice of Assessment after Provincial Assessor approval (~2 weeks).

fees.description: Php 200.00 (Sworn Statement: Php 100.00 + Notary: Php 50.00 + Documentary Stamps: Php 50.00)

plainLanguageName: Declare and assess newly acquired machinery for taxes

service: Application for Newly Declared Real Property (Land)

detailedRequirements.requirement: Photocopy of Identification Card of Owner

clientSteps.action: Present all requirements and fill up Sworn Statement.
Pay corresponding fee at the Treasury Office.
Wait for ocular inspection and FAAS preparation. Note: owner required to pay 10 years back taxes.
Receive Tax Declaration after Provincial Assessor approval (~2 weeks).

plainLanguageName: Declare a new piece of land for real property tax

service: Annotation and Cancellation of Mortgage, Adverse Claim, Hold Transaction and Other Annotations

clientSteps.action: Present all requirements.
Wait for preparation of indorsement letter to Provincial Assessor's Office.
Annotation/Cancellation is done after Provincial Assessor approval (~2 weeks).

clientSteps.action: Submit the Indorsement of Notice of Annotation or Notice of Cancellation of Warrant of Levy.
Wait for verification and annotation/cancellation on FAAS and Tax Declaration.

service: Application for Verification of Property

clientSteps.action: Present request slip for verification and pay corresponding fee at the Treasurer's Office.

detailedRequirements.requirement: Request Letter for cancellation of assessment
Fire Certificate (for property razed by fire)

clientSteps.action: Present and submit all requirements.
Wait for ocular inspection and preparation of Notice of Cancellation.
Receive owner's copy of Notice of Cancellation after Provincial Assessor approval (~2 weeks).

service: Issuance of Certified True Copy of Documents

detailedRequirements.requirement: Authorization Letter if the requestee is not the registered owner

clientSteps.action: Present and submit required documents.
Pay corresponding fee at the Treasurer's Office.
Receive certified requested documents.

fees.description: Php 130.00 (Certified Xerox Copy: Php 100.00 + Documentary Stamps: Php 30.00)

Accept requested supplies after preparation and recording.

Wait for Property Management Section to check availability and prepare PAR and ICS.

Submit PRS form to the GSO authorized representative.
Coordinate with GSO for turn-over of properties to the warehouse.

whoMayAvail: Resigned, retired, transferred, and deceased employees

clientSteps.action: Submit a request for clearance from accountabilities.
Wait for inventory check and inspection of accountable properties.
Receive Clearance from Property Accountability.

whoMayAvail: Municipal employees of Taytay / Authorized drivers

clientSteps.action: Visit GSO and approach the Property Insurance and Registration Unit (PIRU).
Submit required documents for GSIS insurance or LTO registration.
Receive processed documents and registration.

clientSteps.action: Provide Statement of Account (SOA) to GSO Utilities staff.
Documents are endorsed to Administrator's Office, Budget Office, Accounting Office, and Treasury Office for signature and payment.

clientSteps.action: Deliver the food items/goods from the supplier to the General Services Office.
GSO food supplies management section receives, counts, and distributes foods at official activities.

service: Request for Fuel Allocation for Government Equipment and Vehicles

detailedRequirements.requirement: Official Receipt and Certificate of Registration (OR/CR) of the vehicle

clientSteps.action: Submit a letter-request to the General Services Officer requesting fuel allocation approval.
Submit a copy of valid driver's license and OR/CR of the vehicle.
If approved, request is endorsed to the Legal Office for issuance of Fuel Allocation Slip.
Present the signed fuel slip to UNIOIL (located at C6, Taytay, Rizal) for fuel release.

plainLanguageName: Get a fuel allocation slip for a government vehicle

Must specify date, occasion, address, and contact number.
GSO schedules the activity and notifies requestor for confirmation.

clientSteps.action: Fill out the Job Order Form or Request letter and have it signed by the Supervising Head.
GSO building maintenance section assesses and carries out the required repairs.

plainLanguageName: Request repair or maintenance of a municipal building

service: Sound System Services

GSO schedules and sets up the sound system for the event.

Bring the government-issued vehicle to the Motorpool Section.

clientSteps.action: Request for garbage collection as per schedule.

GSO verifies documents and releases impounded vehicle or items to the rightful owner.

service: CCTV Footage Request (Municipal Building Security Section – MBSS)

clientSteps.action: Properly accomplish the request form with: Name, Address, Contact Number, Details and purpose, Date and Time of Incident, and Location.
MBSS personnel reviews the requested CCTV footage (~40 minutes).

plainLanguageName: Request a review of CCTV footage for incident documentation

service: Lost and Found (Municipal Building Security Section – MBSS)

clientSteps.action: Properly accomplish the Lost and Found Form with: Name, Address, Contact Number, Date and Time of Incident, and Location.
Present complete requirements and valid ID with precise item description.
If ownership is proven, receive the found item and sign the blotter logbook.

plainLanguageName: Report a lost item or claim a found item at the Municipal Building

service: GSO Free Water Refilling Station

clientSteps.action: Visit the GSO and approach the personnel-in-charge at the water refilling station.
Staff cleans, inspects, and refills your water bottles.
Receive refilled water bottles.

plainLanguageName: Get free drinking water at the GSO Water Refilling Station

service: Timely Registration of Certificate of Live Birth (Born in Taytay, Rizal)
whoMayAvail: Parents, guardians, hospitals, maternity/lying-in clinics, and birth attendants of registrants born in Taytay

detailedRequirements.requirement: Properly accomplished Municipal Form No. 102 – Certificate of Live Birth (1 set, all original, black ink)
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: Hospital, Maternity/Lying-In Clinics, other birthing facilities

detailedRequirements.requirement: Certificate of Marriage of Parents (if married)
Notarized Affidavit of Admission of Paternity (if child acknowledged – for Illegitimate Child)
Notarized Affidavit to Use the Surname of the Father (if applicable – for Illegitimate Child)

clientSteps.action: Present properly accomplished Municipal Form No. 102 – Certificate of Live Birth and all requirements within 30 days from birth.
LCRO checks completeness of entries and assigns registry number.
Receive released personal copy of the document.

plainLanguageName: Register your baby's birth certificate within 30 days

service: Late Registration of Certificate of Live Birth (Born in Taytay, Rizal)
whoMayAvail: Parents, guardians, and registrants born in Taytay – filing after the 30-day period

detailedRequirements.requirement: Certificate of Live Birth for Late Registration (1 Set)
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: Hospital, Maternity/Lying-In Clinics, other birthing facilities

detailedRequirements.requirement: Personal Appearance of registrant or parent/guardian (as applicable)

For Legitimate Child (17 and below): Certified True Copy of Parents' Certificate of Marriage, Notarized Joint Affidavit of Two Disinterested Persons, Affidavit of Late Registration, Notarized Affidavit of Registrant per PSA MC No. 2024-17A, Form 137/School Records, Philsys National ID/transaction slip, 2x2 ID Photo, Government-issued IDs, Barangay Certification of Residency, Certificate of Death of parent/s (if applicable)

For Illegitimate Child (17 and below): Same as above plus Notarized Affidavit of Admission of Paternity, Notarized Affidavit to Use the Surname of the Father, Notarized Sworn Attestation (for children 7–17)

For Applicants 18 and above: All above plus NBI Clearance, Police Clearance, SSS E-1, Philhealth MDR, GSIS Service Record, Voter's Registration Record, Income Tax Return, Medical Records, Affidavit of Abandonment (if applicable)
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: NBI, PNP, SSS, Philhealth, GSIS, COMELEC

clientSteps.action: Present accomplished COLB form and submit all required documents.
Conduct of interview with the client/parents/registrant.
Registration after 10-day posting period.
Receive owner's copy after posting period.

fees.description: Free (Out-of-town courier: Php 90–150 depending on location)

plainLanguageName: Register a birth certificate after the 30-day deadline

service: Application and Issuance of Marriage License (Residents of Taytay, Rizal)
whoMayAvail: Marriage applicants who are at least 18 years of age, with at least one applicant residing in Taytay

detailedRequirements.requirement: Accomplished Application Form for Marriage License (AML) – 2 Copies
Personal Appearance of both contracting parties
Birth Certificate or Baptismal Certificate (1 Original, 1 Photocopy)
Valid IDs of both applicants (at least one showing residency in Taytay, Rizal)
Certificate of No Marriage (CENOMAR) from PSA for both contracting parties (issued within 6 months)
Pre-Marriage Orientation and Counseling Certificate (Family Planning and Marriage Counseling)
Parental Consent Form (Municipal Form No. 92) for ages 18–20 (if applicable)
Parental Advice (Municipal Form No. 8) for ages 21–25 (if applicable)
Certificate of Legal Capacity to Marry (for foreigners)
Certificate of Death of spouse (for widow/widower)
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: LCRO where death was registered / PSA

detailedRequirements.requirement: Certified True Copy of Decree of Annulment / Absolute Nullity of Marriage (if applicable)

supportingDocumentsDetail.note: Marriage License is valid for 120 days from date of issuance, in any part of the Philippines.

clientSteps.action: Secure marriage license application form, present with required documents, and both applicants will be interviewed.
Pay the corresponding fees (Php 600.00) at the Municipal Treasury Office.
Attend family planning and marriage counseling (PMOC) every Friday morning.
Receive Marriage License after 10-day posting and payment of Municipal Form No. 10 (Php 2.00).

fees.description: Php 600.00 marriage fees + Php 2.00 (Accountable Form No. 54)

plainLanguageName: Apply for a marriage license

service: Timely Registration of Certificate of Marriage (Married in Taytay, Rizal)
whoMayAvail: Contracting parties married in Taytay, Rizal

detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: LCRO where the Marriage License was issued

detailedRequirements.requirement: Request for the Celebration of Marriage in a place other than those authorized by law (if applicable)

clientSteps.action: Submit the Certificate of Marriage for registration.
LCRO registers the Certificate of Marriage and assigns Registry Number.
Receive owner's copy of the Certificate of Marriage.

plainLanguageName: Register a marriage certificate within the prescribed period

service: Late Registration of Certificate of Marriage (Married in Taytay, Rizal)
whoMayAvail: Solemnizing officers and contracting parties whose marriage was not registered within the prescribed period

detailedRequirements.requirement: Duly Accomplished and Signed Certificate of Marriage (Original or Duplicate Copy)
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: Solemnizing Officer / Church / Place of Marriage

detailedRequirements.requirement: Latest CENOMAR from PSA for both contracting parties (within 6 months of submission)
Affidavit of Delayed Registration of Certificate of Marriage executed by the Solemnizing Officer
Sworn Statement/Affidavit of Contracting Parties stating the reason for delay
Certified Copy of Application for Marriage License (if applicable)
Certified True Copy of Birth Certificate of the couple's children with Date and Place of Marriage (if with children)
detailedRequirements.where_to_secure: LCRO where children were born / PSA

detailedRequirements.requirement: Affidavit of Cohabitation (if exempt from Marriage License requirement under Article 34)

clientSteps.action: Submit the signed Certificate of Marriage and all requirements.
Accomplish the new Certificate of Marriage following the information in the old copy (no changes to entries).
Registration after 10-day posting period.
Receive owner's copy after posting period.

plainLanguageName: Register a marriage certificate after the prescribed period

service: Timely Registration of Certificate of Death
whoMayAvail: Funeral parlors and general public whose relatives died in Taytay, Rizal

detailedRequirements.requirement: Duly accomplished Municipal Form No. 103 – Certificate of Death/Fetal Death Form (1 Set)
Certification of Health Officer (Certificate of Death)
Certification of Embalmer (back portion of Certificate of Death)
Post Mortem of Death Certificate (if applicable)

clientSteps.action: Present accomplished Certificate of Death with complete signatures.
Pay Burial/Cremation Permit fee (Php 150.00) at the Municipal Treasury Office (if applicable).
Pay Transfer Permit fee (Php 150.00) at the Municipal Treasury Office (if applicable).
LCRO registers the Certificate of Death and assigns Registry Number.
Receive owner's copy after signing of documents.

service: Animal Bite Treatment Center
description: Anti-rabies vaccination and wound management for individuals bitten by animals.

service: Cash Subsidy for Solo Parents
description: Financial assistance provided semi-annually to qualified solo parents in the municipality.
steps: Register as a Solo Parent with the MSWDO.
Submit required documents during the designated payout period.
Receive cash subsidy (Php 1,000 per month or as allocated).

sources.name: Republic Act No. 11861 (Expanded Solo Parents Welfare Act of 2022)

service: Expanded Centenarian Program Payout (Ages 80, 85, 90, 95)
description: Provision of cash incentives for elderly residents reaching milestone ages of 80, 85, 90, and 95, facilitated by the National Commission of Senior Citizens (NCSC).
steps: Verify if the senior's name is included in the official NCSC Masterlist (January to June 2025 batch).
Secure required templates (Authorization, SPA, or Warranty & Release forms) from the OSCA Office between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM.
Prepare the specific set of documents based on the claimant's status (Personal, Representative, or Deceased).
Proceed to the Municipal Treasury Office on the designated payout date and time.
Submit documents for verification and receive the cash incentive.
requirements: FOR PERSONAL CLAIM: 2 copies of valid ID with signature or thumbmark.
FOR REPRESENTATIVE: Authorization Letter (NCSC template), Physical ID of grantee (original + photocopy), Physical ID of claimant (original + photocopy).
FOR REPRESENTATIVE (BEDRIDDEN): Barangay Certification signed by Captain and BHW or Medical Certificate.
FOR REPRESENTATIVE (PROOF OF LIFE): Half-body photo of the grantee holding a current calendar or newspaper.

FOR DECEASED GRANTEE: Special Power of Attorney (SPA template), Warranty and Release from Liability form, Original or CTC of Death Certificate, Claimant ID with 3 signatures, and Proof of Relationship.

faqs.question: Where can I get the SPA or Authorization templates?
faqs.answer: Templates are available at the Office of the Senior Citizens Affairs (OSCA) from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this specific payout?
faqs.answer: Seniors who are part of the qualified beneficiaries list from January to June 2025 provided by the NCSC.

quickInfo.documents: Valid IDs, SPA/Authorization, Proof of Life

sources.name: Office of the Senior Citizens Affairs (OSCA) Los Baños

service: Fire Safety Inspection Certificate (FSIC)
description: Required for occupancy and business permits to ensure the structure meets fire safety standards.
requirements: Building Permit Number and Date Issued

service: Garbage Collection Schedule
description: Weekly schedule for waste collection per barangay. Please segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste.
steps: Monday & Thursday: Batong Malake, San Antonio, Maahas (Biodegradable)
Tuesday & Friday: Bayog, Mayondon, Malinta (Biodegradable)
Wednesday: All Barangays (Non-Biodegradable / Recyclables)
Saturday: Special collection for Market area
Sunday: No Collection
requirements: Please ensure waste is segregated before putting it out.
Bring out trash only between 6:00 AM - 8:00 AM.

sources.name: Republic Act No. 9003 (Ecological Solid Waste Management Act)

service: Medical Assistance (AICS)
description: Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situations for medical needs.
steps: Submit requirements for screening.
Undergo interview by Social Worker.
Receive Guarantee Letter or Cash Assistance.

sources.name: DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02 Series of 2014

faqs.question: When does enrollment start?
faqs.answer: Enrollment typically begins in late May. Please follow the official DepEd page for announcements.

service: Rural Health Unit (RHU) Locations
description: Locations of health centers providing free primary care, immunization, and maternal services.
steps: RHU 1: Main Health Center, Municipal Hall Compound, Brgy. Timugan
RHU 2: Brgy. Batong Malake (Near Barangay Hall)
RHU 3: Brgy. Mayondon (Purok 3)
Animal Bite Center: Municipal Hall Compound
